revision.comment: Auto generating page. date placed far in the past so it is attributed to Immanuelle and FireflyBot sends them messages if it is ever imported to Wikipedia.
1 revision imported: importing redirects I created with chatGPT

revision.text:
{{Documentation subpage}} : ''This template is part of [[Wikipedia:Wikimedia sister projects|Wikimedia sister projects]].'' The {{tl|Wikisourcecat}} template links to a "Category:" name in the [[Wikisource]] repository (edited using a Wikimedia login name). If that category name is misspelled or missing, the link will still appear "[[blue]]" even though the link will fail. Check spelling of names carefully. == Usage == 
{{Wikisourcecat|related Wikisource category (optional)|Wikipedia name (optional)}}
Both parameters are optional, and are intended for cases where the Wikisource category name is different from the Wikipedia page name. The first parameter is the name of the corresponding category on the Wikisource, ''without'' the Category: prefix. If this first parameter is used, then the template will display the Wikisource category name, which may be confusing to readers. This text can be replaced by the second parameter, where the Wikipedia page name would typically be used. For example, on the [[Polar bear]] page, :{{Wikisourcecat|Ursus maritimus|Polar bears}} produces a link to [[Wikisource:Category:Ursus maritimus]] displayed as [[Wikisource:Category:Ursus maritimus|Polar bears]]. ==Location== [[File:Sister link template columns problem.jpg|thumb|If using a large, graphical template produces odd layout problems, like the excessive white space shown on the right of this screenshot, then switch to a different sister template.]] {{cross}} '''Do not place this template in a section all by itself.''' {{cross}} '''Do not place this template in a section containing columns.''' This template should normally be placed at the ''top'' of the ==External links== section at the end of an article, if the article has a section for [[WP:EL|external links]]. If no such section exists, then please place it at the top of the last section in the article. On [[WP:DAB|disambiguation pages]], this template should be listed above other text, but below any cleanup templates, images, infoboxes or navboxes. See [[Wikipedia:Wikimedia sister projects#Where to place links]] for more information and alternatives. == See also == * {{tl|Wikisource}} {{sandbox other|| [[Category:Interwiki link sidebar templates]] [[Category:Interwiki category linking templates]] [[Category:Wikisource link templates|Category]] }}

revision.comment: Bot: convert local/ILL link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
{{Documentation subpage}} : ''This template is part of [[Wikipedia:Wikimedia sister projects|Wikimedia sister projects]].'' The {{tl|Wikisourcecat}} template links to a "Category:" name in the [[Wikisource]] repository (edited using a Wikimedia login name). If that category name is misspelled or missing, the link will still appear "[[:en:Blue|Blue]]" even though the link will fail. Check spelling of names carefully. == Usage == 
{{Wikisourcecat|related Wikisource category (optional)|Wikipedia name (optional)}}
Both parameters are optional, and are intended for cases where the Wikisource category name is different from the Wikipedia page name. The first parameter is the name of the corresponding category on the Wikisource, ''without'' the Category: prefix. If this first parameter is used, then the template will display the Wikisource category name, which may be confusing to readers. This text can be replaced by the second parameter, where the Wikipedia page name would typically be used. For example, on the [[Polar bear]] page, :{{Wikisourcecat|Ursus maritimus|Polar bears}} produces a link to [[Wikisource:Category:Ursus maritimus]] displayed as [[Wikisource:Category:Ursus maritimus|Polar bears]]. ==Location== [[File:Sister link template columns problem.jpg|thumb|If using a large, graphical template produces odd layout problems, like the excessive white space shown on the right of this screenshot, then switch to a different sister template.]] {{cross}} '''Do not place this template in a section all by itself.''' {{cross}} '''Do not place this template in a section containing columns.''' This template should normally be placed at the ''top'' of the ==External links== section at the end of an article, if the article has a section for [[WP:EL|external links]]. If no such section exists, then please place it at the top of the last section in the article. On [[WP:DAB|disambiguation pages]], this template should be listed above other text, but below any cleanup templates, images, infoboxes or navboxes. See [[Wikipedia:Wikimedia sister projects#Where to place links]] for more information and alternatives. == See also == * {{tl|Wikisource}} {{sandbox other|| [[Category:Interwiki link sidebar templates]] [[Category:Interwiki category linking templates]] [[Category:Wikisource link templates|Category]] }}

revision.text:
{{Documentation subpage}} : ''This template is part of [[Wikipedia:Wikimedia sister projects|Wikimedia sister projects]].'' The {{tl|Wikisourcecat}} template links to a "Category:" name in the [[Wikisource]] repository (edited using a Wikimedia login name). If that category name is misspelled or missing, the link will still appear "[[:en:Blue|Blue]]" even though the link will fail. Check spelling of names carefully. == Usage == 
{{Wikisourcecat|related Wikisource category (optional)|Wikipedia name (optional)}}
Both parameters are optional, and are intended for cases where the Wikisource category name is different from the Wikipedia page name. The first parameter is the name of the corresponding category on the Wikisource, ''without'' the Category: prefix. If this first parameter is used, then the template will display the Wikisource category name, which may be confusing to readers. This text can be replaced by the second parameter, where the Wikipedia page name would typically be used. For example, on the [[:en:Polar bear|Polar bear]] page, :{{Wikisourcecat|Ursus maritimus|Polar bears}} produces a link to [[Wikisource:Category:Ursus maritimus]] displayed as [[Wikisource:Category:Ursus maritimus|Polar bears]]. ==Location== [[File:Sister link template columns problem.jpg|thumb|If using a large, graphical template produces odd layout problems, like the excessive white space shown on the right of this screenshot, then switch to a different sister template.]] {{cross}} '''Do not place this template in a section all by itself.''' {{cross}} '''Do not place this template in a section containing columns.''' This template should normally be placed at the ''top'' of the ==External links== section at the end of an article, if the article has a section for [[WP:EL|external links]]. If no such section exists, then please place it at the top of the last section in the article. On [[WP:DAB|disambiguation pages]], this template should be listed above other text, but below any cleanup templates, images, infoboxes or navboxes. See [[Wikipedia:Wikimedia sister projects#Where to place links]] for more information and alternatives. == See also == * {{tl|Wikisource}} {{sandbox other|| [[Category:Interwiki link sidebar templates]] [[Category:Interwiki category linking templates]] [[Category:Wikisource link templates|Category]] }}

revision.text:
{{Documentation subpage}} : ''This template is part of [[Wikipedia:Wikimedia sister projects|Wikimedia sister projects]].'' The {{tl|Wikisourcecat}} template links to a "Category:" name in the [[:en:Wikisource|Wikisource]] repository (edited using a Wikimedia login name). If that category name is misspelled or missing, the link will still appear "[[:en:Blue|Blue]]" even though the link will fail. Check spelling of names carefully. == Usage == 
{{Wikisourcecat|related Wikisource category (optional)|Wikipedia name (optional)}}
Both parameters are optional, and are intended for cases where the Wikisource category name is different from the Wikipedia page name. The first parameter is the name of the corresponding category on the Wikisource, ''without'' the Category: prefix. If this first parameter is used, then the template will display the Wikisource category name, which may be confusing to readers. This text can be replaced by the second parameter, where the Wikipedia page name would typically be used. For example, on the [[:en:Polar bear|Polar bear]] page, :{{Wikisourcecat|Ursus maritimus|Polar bears}} produces a link to [[Wikisource:Category:Ursus maritimus]] displayed as [[Wikisource:Category:Ursus maritimus|Polar bears]]. ==Location== [[File:Sister link template columns problem.jpg|thumb|If using a large, graphical template produces odd layout problems, like the excessive white space shown on the right of this screenshot, then switch to a different sister template.]] {{cross}} '''Do not place this template in a section all by itself.''' {{cross}} '''Do not place this template in a section containing columns.''' This template should normally be placed at the ''top'' of the ==External links== section at the end of an article, if the article has a section for [[WP:EL|external links]]. If no such section exists, then please place it at the top of the last section in the article. On [[WP:DAB|disambiguation pages]], this template should be listed above other text, but below any cleanup templates, images, infoboxes or navboxes. See [[Wikipedia:Wikimedia sister projects#Where to place links]] for more information and alternatives. == See also == * {{tl|Wikisource}} {{sandbox other|| [[Category:Interwiki link sidebar templates]] [[Category:Interwiki category linking templates]] [[Category:Wikisource link templates|Category]] }}

revision.comment: Bot: add {{wikidata link|Q...}} or [[Category:Templates without wikidata]]
revision.text:
{{Documentation subpage}} : ''This template is part of [[Wikipedia:Wikimedia sister projects|Wikimedia sister projects]].'' The {{tl|Wikisourcecat}} template links to a "Category:" name in the [[:en:Wikisource|Wikisource]] repository (edited using a Wikimedia login name). If that category name is misspelled or missing, the link will still appear "[[:en:Blue|Blue]]" even though the link will fail. Check spelling of names carefully. == Usage == 
{{Wikisourcecat|related Wikisource category (optional)|Wikipedia name (optional)}}
Both parameters are optional, and are intended for cases where the Wikisource category name is different from the Wikipedia page name. The first parameter is the name of the corresponding category on the Wikisource, ''without'' the Category: prefix. If this first parameter is used, then the template will display the Wikisource category name, which may be confusing to readers. This text can be replaced by the second parameter, where the Wikipedia page name would typically be used. For example, on the [[:en:Polar bear|Polar bear]] page, :{{Wikisourcecat|Ursus maritimus|Polar bears}} produces a link to [[Wikisource:Category:Ursus maritimus]] displayed as [[Wikisource:Category:Ursus maritimus|Polar bears]]. ==Location== [[File:Sister link template columns problem.jpg|thumb|If using a large, graphical template produces odd layout problems, like the excessive white space shown on the right of this screenshot, then switch to a different sister template.]] {{cross}} '''Do not place this template in a section all by itself.''' {{cross}} '''Do not place this template in a section containing columns.''' This template should normally be placed at the ''top'' of the ==External links== section at the end of an article, if the article has a section for [[WP:EL|external links]]. If no such section exists, then please place it at the top of the last section in the article. On [[WP:DAB|disambiguation pages]], this template should be listed above other text, but below any cleanup templates, images, infoboxes or navboxes. See [[Wikipedia:Wikimedia sister projects#Where to place links]] for more information and alternatives. == See also == * {{tl|Wikisource}} {{sandbox other|| [[Category:Interwiki link sidebar templates]] [[Category:Interwiki category linking templates]] [[Category:Wikisource link templates|Category]] }} [[Category:Templates without wikidata]]

revision.comment: imported only latest revision from https://archive.org/details/miraheze-wiki-shintowiki-20251218 to avoid excessive disk usage, comment on the revision was Text replacement - "↵<noinclude>[[Category:Templates without wikidata]]</noinclude>" to ""
revision.text:
{{Documentation subpage}} : ''This template is part of [[Wikipedia:Wikimedia sister projects|Wikimedia sister projects]].'' The {{tl|Wikisourcecat}} template links to a "Category:" name in the [[:en:Wikisource|Wikisource]] repository (edited using a Wikimedia login name). If that category name is misspelled or missing, the link will still appear "[[:en:Blue|Blue]]" even though the link will fail. Check spelling of names carefully. == Usage == 
{{Wikisourcecat|related Wikisource category (optional)|Wikipedia name (optional)}}
Both parameters are optional, and are intended for cases where the Wikisource category name is different from the Wikipedia page name. The first parameter is the name of the corresponding category on the Wikisource, ''without'' the Category: prefix. If this first parameter is used, then the template will display the Wikisource category name, which may be confusing to readers. This text can be replaced by the second parameter, where the Wikipedia page name would typically be used. For example, on the [[:en:Polar bear|Polar bear]] page, :{{Wikisourcecat|Ursus maritimus|Polar bears}} produces a link to [[Wikisource:Category:Ursus maritimus]] displayed as [[Wikisource:Category:Ursus maritimus|Polar bears]]. ==Location== [[File:Sister link template columns problem.jpg|thumb|If using a large, graphical template produces odd layout problems, like the excessive white space shown on the right of this screenshot, then switch to a different sister template.]] {{cross}} '''Do not place this template in a section all by itself.''' {{cross}} '''Do not place this template in a section containing columns.''' This template should normally be placed at the ''top'' of the ==External links== section at the end of an article, if the article has a section for [[WP:EL|external links]]. If no such section exists, then please place it at the top of the last section in the article. On [[WP:DAB|disambiguation pages]], this template should be listed above other text, but below any cleanup templates, images, infoboxes or navboxes. See [[Wikipedia:Wikimedia sister projects#Where to place links]] for more information and alternatives. == See also == * {{tl|Wikisource}} {{sandbox other|| [[Category:Interwiki link sidebar templates]] [[Category:Interwiki category linking templates]] [[Category:Wikisource link templates|Category]] }}

revision.comment: Bot: Adding dummy category to make categories work after wiki restoration
revision.text:
{{Documentation subpage}} : ''This template is part of [[Wikipedia:Wikimedia sister projects|Wikimedia sister projects]].'' The {{tl|Wikisourcecat}} template links to a "Category:" name in the [[:en:Wikisource|Wikisource]] repository (edited using a Wikimedia login name). If that category name is misspelled or missing, the link will still appear "[[:en:Blue|Blue]]" even though the link will fail. Check spelling of names carefully. == Usage == 
{{Wikisourcecat|related Wikisource category (optional)|Wikipedia name (optional)}}
Both parameters are optional, and are intended for cases where the Wikisource category name is different from the Wikipedia page name. The first parameter is the name of the corresponding category on the Wikisource, ''without'' the Category: prefix. If this first parameter is used, then the template will display the Wikisource category name, which may be confusing to readers. This text can be replaced by the second parameter, where the Wikipedia page name would typically be used. For example, on the [[:en:Polar bear|Polar bear]] page, :{{Wikisourcecat|Ursus maritimus|Polar bears}} produces a link to [[Wikisource:Category:Ursus maritimus]] displayed as [[Wikisource:Category:Ursus maritimus|Polar bears]]. ==Location== [[File:Sister link template columns problem.jpg|thumb|If using a large, graphical template produces odd layout problems, like the excessive white space shown on the right of this screenshot, then switch to a different sister template.]] {{cross}} '''Do not place this template in a section all by itself.''' {{cross}} '''Do not place this template in a section containing columns.''' This template should normally be placed at the ''top'' of the ==External links== section at the end of an article, if the article has a section for [[WP:EL|external links]]. If no such section exists, then please place it at the top of the last section in the article. On [[WP:DAB|disambiguation pages]], this template should be listed above other text, but below any cleanup templates, images, infoboxes or navboxes. See [[Wikipedia:Wikimedia sister projects#Where to place links]] for more information and alternatives. == See also == * {{tl|Wikisource}} {{sandbox other|| [[Category:Interwiki link sidebar templates]] [[Category:Interwiki category linking templates]] [[Category:Wikisource link templates|Category]] }} [[Category:Templates]]

revision.comment: Bot: wrap 4 stray tag(s) in noinclude [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23694499169|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
{{Documentation subpage}} : ''This template is part of [[Wikipedia:Wikimedia sister projects|Wikimedia sister projects]].'' The {{tl|Wikisourcecat}} template links to a "Category:" name in the [[:en:Wikisource|Wikisource]] repository (edited using a Wikimedia login name). If that category name is misspelled or missing, the link will still appear "[[:en:Blue|Blue]]" even though the link will fail. Check spelling of names carefully. == Usage == 
{{Wikisourcecat|related Wikisource category (optional)|Wikipedia name (optional)}}
Both parameters are optional, and are intended for cases where the Wikisource category name is different from the Wikipedia page name. The first parameter is the name of the corresponding category on the Wikisource, ''without'' the Category: prefix. If this first parameter is used, then the template will display the Wikisource category name, which may be confusing to readers. This text can be replaced by the second parameter, where the Wikipedia page name would typically be used. For example, on the [[:en:Polar bear|Polar bear]] page, :{{Wikisourcecat|Ursus maritimus|Polar bears}} produces a link to [[Wikisource:Category:Ursus maritimus]] displayed as [[Wikisource:Category:Ursus maritimus|Polar bears]]. ==Location== [[File:Sister link template columns problem.jpg|thumb|If using a large, graphical template produces odd layout problems, like the excessive white space shown on the right of this screenshot, then switch to a different sister template.]] {{cross}} '''Do not place this template in a section all by itself.''' {{cross}} '''Do not place this template in a section containing columns.''' This template should normally be placed at the ''top'' of the ==External links== section at the end of an article, if the article has a section for [[WP:EL|external links]]. If no such section exists, then please place it at the top of the last section in the article. On [[WP:DAB|disambiguation pages]], this template should be listed above other text, but below any cleanup templates, images, infoboxes or navboxes. See [[Wikipedia:Wikimedia sister projects#Where to place links]] for more information and alternatives. == See also == * {{tl|Wikisource}} {{sandbox other|| }} [[Category:Interwiki link sidebar templates]] [[Category:Interwiki category linking templates]] [[Category:Wikisource link templates|Category]] [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]]

revision.comment: Bot: strip HTML comments [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24999656626|time triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
{{Documentation subpage}} : ''This template is part of [[Wikipedia:Wikimedia sister projects|Wikimedia sister projects]].'' The {{tl|Wikisourcecat}} template links to a "Category:" name in the [[:en:Wikisource|Wikisource]] repository (edited using a Wikimedia login name). If that category name is misspelled or missing, the link will still appear "[[:en:Blue|Blue]]" even though the link will fail. Check spelling of names carefully. == Usage == 
{{Wikisourcecat|related Wikisource category (optional)|Wikipedia name (optional)}}
Both parameters are optional, and are intended for cases where the Wikisource category name is different from the Wikipedia page name. The first parameter is the name of the corresponding category on the Wikisource, ''without'' the Category: prefix. If this first parameter is used, then the template will display the Wikisource category name, which may be confusing to readers. This text can be replaced by the second parameter, where the Wikipedia page name would typically be used. For example, on the [[:en:Polar bear|Polar bear]] page, :{{Wikisourcecat|Ursus maritimus|Polar bears}} produces a link to [[Wikisource:Category:Ursus maritimus]] displayed as [[Wikisource:Category:Ursus maritimus|Polar bears]]. ==Location== [[File:Sister link template columns problem.jpg|thumb|If using a large, graphical template produces odd layout problems, like the excessive white space shown on the right of this screenshot, then switch to a different sister template.]] {{cross}} '''Do not place this template in a section all by itself.''' {{cross}} '''Do not place this template in a section containing columns.''' This template should normally be placed at the ''top'' of the ==External links== section at the end of an article, if the article has a section for [[WP:EL|external links]]. If no such section exists, then please place it at the top of the last section in the article. On [[WP:DAB|disambiguation pages]], this template should be listed above other text, but below any cleanup templates, images, infoboxes or navboxes. See [[Wikipedia:Wikimedia sister projects#Where to place links]] for more information and alternatives. == See also == * {{tl|Wikisource}} {{sandbox other|| }} [[Category:Interwiki link sidebar templates]] [[Category:Interwiki category linking templates]] [[Category:Wikisource link templates|Category]] [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]]
